channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Researchers are building AI-powered robot labs. What does this mean for science?
item.description: Thanks to new technologies like artificial intelligence, scientists are increasingly freed from the constraints of the laboratory. It raises questions about how much humans should outsource to robots.
item.content:encoded: Thanks to new technologies like artificial intelligence, scientists are increasingly freed from the constraints of the laboratory. It raises questions about how much humans should outsource to robots.
item.dc:creator: Katia Riddle

item.title: Sonny Rollins, a giant of jazz saxophone, had a lifelong commitment to spontaneity
item.description: Sonny Rollins, the jazz legend known as the Saxophone Colossus died yesterday at his home in Woodstock, N.Y. He was 95 years old and one of the last of his generation of jazz titans.
item.content:encoded: Sonny Rollins, the jazz legend known as the Saxophone Colossus died yesterday at his home in Woodstock, N.Y. He was 95 years old and one of the last of his generation of jazz titans.
item.dc:creator: Nate Chinen

item.title: Ecuador fishermen claim U.S. strike destroyed boats and crew detained
item.description: Fishermen working off Ecuador say they were attacked by drones, taken onboard a U.S.-flagged vessel, cuffed and handed over to Salvadoran officials. They want to know who attacked them and why.
item.content:encoded: Fishermen working off Ecuador say they were attacked by drones, taken onboard a U.S.-flagged vessel, cuffed and handed over to Salvadoran officials. They want to know who attacked them and why.
item.dc:creator: Carrie Kahn

item.title: 'We'll never know if he could have been saved.' The gaps in Trump's rural health fund
item.description: Republicans are talking up healthcare amid the midterm election campaigns, but President Trump's $50 billion fund for rural healthcare won't reopen hospitals.
item.content:encoded: Republicans are talking up healthcare amid the midterm election campaigns, but President Trump's $50 billion fund for rural healthcare won't reopen hospitals.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Jane Tribble

item.title: The challenges to providing aid in this latest Ebola outbreak
item.description: NPR's Sacha Pfeiffer talks with Heather Kerr of the International Rescue Committee about the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo and the challenges facing the response efforts.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Sacha Pfeiffer talks with Heather Kerr of the International Rescue Committee about the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo and the challenges facing the response efforts.
item.dc:creator: Kathryn Fink

item.title: Texas Republicans will choose a candidate for general election Senate contest Tuesday
item.description: Some Texas Republicans worry that the intraparty Senate primary fight could make it difficult for to unite behind Tuesday's winner to defeat a potentially strong Democratic challenge in the midterms.
item.content:encoded: Some Texas Republicans worry that the intraparty Senate primary fight could make it difficult for to unite behind Tuesday's winner to defeat a potentially strong Democratic challenge in the midterms.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Schneider

item.title: U.S. announces official 2026 FIFA World Cup roster
item.description: The U.S. National Men's Soccer Team announced it's official 26-player roster for the World Cup. The tournament starts in June.
item.content:encoded: The U.S. National Men's Soccer Team announced it's official 26-player roster for the World Cup. The tournament starts in June.
item.dc:creator: Jeffrey Pierre

item.title: Hartford Whalers fans keep team spirit alive nearly 30 years after move
item.description: The Hartford Whalers left Connecticut nearly 30 years ago, but Whalers merch still sells briskly even though the team is now the Carolina Hurricanes.
item.content:encoded: The Hartford Whalers left Connecticut nearly 30 years ago, but Whalers merch still sells briskly even though the team is now the Carolina Hurricanes.
item.dc:creator: Abigail Brone

item.title: NY Knicks fans are celebrating a sports comeback almost 30 years in the making
item.description: The New York Knicks are heading to the NBA finals for the first time since 1999. NPR's Scott Detrow speaks with Wall Street Journal sports and humor columnist Jason Gay about the comeback.
item.content:encoded: The New York Knicks are heading to the NBA finals for the first time since 1999. NPR's Scott Detrow speaks with Wall Street Journal sports and humor columnist Jason Gay about the comeback.
item.dc:creator: Jason Fuller

item.title: As Trump touts progress on Iran talks, major obstacles remain
item.description: President Trump heralded an advance in making a deal with Iran to end the war, but the way forward remains unclear.
item.content:encoded: President Trump heralded an advance in making a deal with Iran to end the war, but the way forward remains unclear.
item.dc:creator: Aya Batrawy
